Product Docs Pricing Compare
Star on GitHub Get started

Compare

Recurso vs Stripe Billing

Stripe's gateway ubiquity is unmatched — and Recurso happily uses Stripe as a gateway. The comparison here is the billing layer: Stripe Billing adds a percentage of billing volume on top of processing, and locks your billing logic into their platform. Recurso is open-source billing you own, on top of whichever gateway you like.

Start self-hosting Read the quickstart

MIT licensed · Go + PostgreSQL + TigerBeetle · Works with Stripe & Razorpay

At a glance

Own your billing layer vs rent it

Stripe gives you unmatched gateway reach and a polished managed product. Recurso gives you the billing engine itself — self-hosted, MIT, gateway-agnostic.

Dimension Recurso Stripe Billing
Model Open source (MIT), self-hosted billing engine Proprietary billing on top of Stripe's platform
Billing-layer fee None$0 software fee; you still pay your gateway's processing fees ~0.7% of billing volumeOn top of processing — see the fee stack below
Gateway ubiquity Works with Stripe, Razorpay, and others — you choose Unbeatable global gateway reach — Stripe's core strength
Lock-in None — own the code, switch gateways freely Billing logic and data tied to the Stripe platform
Self-hosting / data residency Full — run in your own VPC Vendor-hosted
Accounting ledger Built-in double-entry ledger; optional TigerBeetle mirror Reporting & Revenue Recognition products (paid add-ons)
India compliance GST e-invoicing (IRP), RBI e-mandates with 24-hour pre-debit notices, DPDP consent — built in Supported in-region, but you're on the Stripe platform's terms
Dunning Algorithmic retries (epsilon-greedy, Thompson, UCB1) with recovered-revenue attribution Smart Retries and recovery tooling, well-proven
Developer experience OpenAPI 3.1 (113 paths), Node + Python SDKs Best-in-class API and docs — a genuine benchmark
Maturity New project, small community Mature, ubiquitous, deeply supported

Fee figures reflect Stripe's publicly listed pricing as of mid-2026 and vary by product, region, and negotiated terms. Confirm current rates directly with Stripe.

Total cost of ownership

The fee stack, and what it blends to

Stripe's headline processing rate isn't the whole story for global SaaS — the fees stack.

Fee component Stripe (typical published rates) Recurso
Billing product fee ~0.7% of billing volume $0
Card processing 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction Your gateway's rate (paid either way)
Tax (if using Stripe Tax) ~+0.5% TaxJar behind a key / static EU VAT table
International card ~+1.5% Your gateway's rate
Currency conversion ~+1% Your gateway's rate
Blended, global SaaS ~4.5%–7.8% of volume Gateway processing only, no billing-layer surcharge

Honest framing: you pay gateway processing fees with Recurso too — those don't disappear, and Stripe can even be your gateway. What Recurso removes is the billing-layer surcharge (~0.7% of volume) and the add-on stacking on top of it. In exchange you take on hosting and ops, and you give up Stripe's unmatched gateway ubiquity and its best-in-class managed experience. For a global business at scale the volume-based surcharge is often the largest avoidable line item — but Stripe's reach is a real reason many teams pay it.

The honest verdict

Which one should you run?

Choose Stripe Billing if…

  • You want the most ubiquitous gateway and a fully managed billing product in one
  • Best-in-class API, docs, and a huge integration ecosystem are worth the premium
  • You'd rather not run infrastructure and the fee stack is acceptable at your scale
  • Speed to launch matters more than owning the billing layer or avoiding lock-in

Choose Recurso if…

  • You want to avoid a percentage-of-volume billing surcharge and fee stacking
  • You want to stay gateway-agnostic — use Stripe, Razorpay, or others, and switch freely
  • You need to self-host, own the code, and keep billing data in your own VPC
  • You want a double-entry ledger and India-native GST / RBI / DPDP compliance built in
  • You have the engineering capacity to run your own billing stack
vs Lago vs Flexprice vs Kill Bill vs Chargebee

Keep Stripe as your gateway. Own the billing.

Free forever on your own infrastructure. Clone the repo and you're minutes away from a seeded dashboard.

Start self-hosting Read the quickstart

git clone https://github.com/swapnull-in/recur-so.git && make demo

recurso

The open-source billing engine for SaaS. Built with Go, PostgreSQL, and TigerBeetle. MIT licensed.
